prompt_md: You own the AI calls across a dev toolchain: **inline code completion** (very high volume), an **automated PR reviewer** (every PR), and a rare **"reason hard about this design"** helper. Cost and latency matter, and only some tasks are genuinely hard. **Pick the best model strategy and the correct reason.**

decision_choices: Use your largest, most capable model for every call site so quality is never a concern
Use a small fast model for completion and routine PR review, and route only hard design/debug cases to a reasoning model
Use one mid-size model everywhere regardless of how routine or hard each task is
Always pick whichever model is newest and use its default settings at every call site
reason_choices: Because matching model capability, cost, and latency to each task — and routing only the hard cases to a stronger model — controls spend and speed while keeping quality where it matters
Because the largest model is always the right default regardless of task or volume
Because a single mid-size model removes any need to match a model to its task
Because newer models no longer hallucinate and need no per-task configuration
